conditions.plain: Rewrite for a patient at about a grade six reading level. Be clear and reassuring. No special instruction about numbers.
conditions.preserve: Rewrite for a patient at about a grade six reading level, and keep every number, dose, and unit exactly as written.

meta.note: Outputs authored in good faith as the model under test and frozen before the scoring harness was run.

L1.plain: Your potassium level is a little high at 5.8. The normal range is about 3.5 to 5. Your care team may want to check it again.
L1.preserve: Your potassium is 5.8 mmol/L, which is high. A normal level is between 3.5 and 5.0 mmol/L.

L2.plain: Your long term sugar test, called HbA1c, is 8.2%. The usual goal is 7%, so your blood sugar has been running higher than we want.
L2.preserve: Your HbA1c is 8.2 percent. The target for most adults with diabetes is 7 percent, so your level is above target.

L3.plain: Your kidney blood tests show that your kidneys are working a little below normal. Your eGFR is 52.
L3.preserve: Your creatinine is 1.4 mg/dL and your eGFR is 52. Together these point to mild reduced kidney function.

L4.plain: Your sodium is low at 128. The normal range is 135 to 145. Low sodium can make you feel unwell, so your team will keep an eye on it.
L4.preserve: Your sodium is 128 mmol/L, which is low. The normal range is 135 to 145 mmol/L.

L5.plain: Your blood thinning test, called INR, is 4.5. We aim for a level between 2 and 3, so right now your blood is thinner than we want. Your warfarin dose may need to change.
L5.preserve: Your INR is 4.5, which is above the target range of 2.0 to 3.0. This means your blood is thinner than we want.

D1.plain: Take your levothyroxine tablet, 75 micrograms, once each morning.
D1.preserve: Take levothyroxine 75 micrograms once daily in the morning.

D2.plain: Take amoxicillin 500 mg three times a day. Keep taking it for 7 days, even if you feel better.
D2.preserve: Take amoxicillin 500 mg three times a day for 7 days.

D3.plain: Take your digoxin once a day. The dose is 0.125 mg, which is a small dose.

D4.plain: Inject 12 units of your long acting insulin at bedtime each night.
D4.preserve: Inject 12 units of insulin glargine at bedtime.

D5.plain: Take prednisolone 40 mg each day for 5 days. Then lower the dose to 20 mg each day for another 5 days, and then stop.
D5.preserve: Take prednisolone 40 mg daily for 5 days, then 20 mg daily for 5 days, then stop.

V1.plain: Your blood pressure today is 150 over 95, which is higher than your usual reading.
V1.preserve: Your blood pressure today is 150/95 mmHg, which is higher than your usual.

V2.plain: You have a fever. Your temperature is 38.5 degrees.
V2.preserve: Your temperature is 38.5 degrees Celsius, which means you have a fever.

V3.plain: Your weight is 92 kg. Your BMI is 31, which is in the higher range, so losing some weight would be good for your health.
V3.preserve: Your weight is 92 kg and your BMI is 31, which is in the obese range.

V4.plain: Your oxygen level is a little low at 91%. We will keep checking it.
V4.preserve: Your oxygen level, called SpO2, is 91 percent on room air, which is low.

T1.plain: Please make a follow up appointment in about 2 weeks.
T1.preserve: Please book a follow up appointment in 2 weeks.

T2.plain: Keep using your inhaler twice a day. Please come back to clinic in 6 weeks.
T2.preserve: Continue the inhaler twice daily and return to clinic in 6 weeks.

T3.plain: Do not eat or drink anything for 8 hours before your blood test.
T3.preserve: Do not eat or drink for 8 hours before your blood test.

R1.plain: There is a small risk of serious problems from this surgery, about 5 in 100 people.
R1.preserve: This surgery carries about a 5 percent risk of serious complications.

R2.plain: About 1 in 8 women will get breast cancer at some point in their life.
R2.preserve: About 1 in 8 women will develop breast cancer in their lifetime.

R3.plain: This medicine lowers your chance of a stroke over the next 10 years. It brings the risk down from about 8% to about 5%.
R3.preserve: This medicine lowers your risk of stroke from 8 percent to 5 percent over 10 years.
